response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong software craftsmanship, excellent communication skills, and some fintech exposure (RIDE Capital), they critically lack the mandatory 5-10 years of professional C++ experience on Windows. The resume shows only ~6 months of C++ work at National Instruments (2014-2015), with the subsequent 10+ years focused on Node.js/TypeScript, DLang, and web technologies. Domain expertise in capital markets or fixed income is also absent, and Windows platform expertise is not evident. This is a fundamental skills mismatch for the role's core requirements.
strengths: Strong communicator with proven ability to teach (workshops, talks, courses) and document ideas
Deep software craftsmanship background with emphasis on TDD, pair programming, CI/CD pipelines, and code quality
Demonstrated investment/fintech domain exposure through RIDE Capital role and ability to rapidly acquire new technical domains
concerns: Severely insufficient C++ experience—only ~6 months in professional settings over 15-year career; primary expertise is Node.js/TypeScript
No evidence of Windows platform specialization or SQL Server/Oracle depth; background is primarily Linux and cloud-native systems
Missing mandatory domain expertise: no fixed-income derivatives, capital-markets systems, or investment-banking experience beyond one brief fintech role
